title: ferray-ufunc: no reduce/accumulate/outer/at/reduceat as generic ufunc methods
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy ufuncs support .reduce(axis), .accumulate(axis), .outer(a,b), .at(a,indices,b), .reduceat(a,indices). These work for ANY ufunc automatically. ferray-ufunc only has add_reduce, add_accumulate, multiply_outer — three hand-written special cases. There's no subtract.reduce, no multiply.accumulate, no power.outer, etc. Without a ufunc object (#376), each combination must be written manually. At minimum, add generic reduce_axis(arr, axis, identity, op) and accumulate_axis(arr, axis, op) that work for any binary function.
